homepage.heroHeadline: Trusted Auto Body Repair in Milwaukee
homepage.heroSubline: Legacy Auto Body delivers honest, high-quality repairs for Milwaukee drivers. Serving Wauwatosa and Brookfield with local expertise since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Legacy Auto Body started as a small shop in Milwaukee's Third Ward. Our founder, a Milwaukee native, saw the need for transparent auto repairs in a city where winter salt and potholes take a toll.
We approach every repair with the same care we'd give our own vehicles—no upsells, just straightforward assessments and durable fixes built for Wisconsin's climate.
When we're not in the shop, you'll find our team supporting local car shows at the Milwaukee Art Museum or volunteering at Bay View's community garage.

valueProps.title: Winter-Ready Repairs
valueProps.description: We use corrosion-resistant materials and techniques to combat Milwaukee's salt-heavy winters.

valueProps.title: Local Knowledge
valueProps.description: Our team understands how Milwaukee's lake-effect snow and freeze-thaw cycles impact vehicles.

valueProps.title: No Hidden Costs
valueProps.description: You'll get a detailed estimate before we touch your car—no surprises when you pick it up.

valueProps.title: Lifetime Guarantee
valueProps.description: All paintwork comes with a lifetime warranty against peeling or fading.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-AUT-R94 or stop by our Milwaukee shop for a no-pressure consultation.

services.name: Collision Repair
services.shortDescription: Restore your vehicle's structure and safety after accidents.
services.description:
Milwaukee's mix of urban traffic and winter road conditions leads to more fender benders than most cities. Our collision repairs go beyond cosmetics—we use frame straightening equipment to restore factory specifications, critical for modern safety systems.

We specialize in repairing salt-induced corrosion around impact points, a common issue in Wauwatosa and Brookfield vehicles. Every repair includes anti-corrosion treatments to prevent future rust, especially important near wheel wells and undercarriages.

From Third Ward parallel parking scrapes to highway collisions, we work with all major insurers while keeping your best interests in mind.
process: Step 1: Free damage assessment with digital imaging
Step 2: Disassembly and hidden damage inspection
Step 3: Frame correction using computerized measuring
Step 4: Reassembly with OEM or certified aftermarket parts
benefits: Factory-spec alignment for proper safety system function

faq.question: How long does a typical collision repair take?
faq.answer: Most repairs take 3-7 days depending on parts availability. We prioritize getting Milwaukee commuters back on the road quickly without cutting corners.

faq.question: Do you work with my insurance?
faq.answer: We're preferred providers for most major insurers in Wisconsin and handle all paperwork. You'll never pay out-of-pocket unless you choose upgrades.

services.name: Paintless Dent Repair
services.shortDescription: Remove dents without repainting, preserving your factory finish.
services.description:
Hailstorms from Lake Michigan and shopping cart dings in Brookfield's parking lots don't have to mean a full repaint. Our technicians use specialized tools to massage dents out from behind, keeping your original paint intact.

This method works best on recent damage before metal fatigues—especially important for Milwaukee's older vehicles where factory paint matches are hard to replicate. We can typically repair dents up to the size of a baseball without filler or repainting.

For East Side residents dealing with tight street parking or Wauwatosa drivers facing grocery store dings, PDR saves 50-75% compared to conventional repairs while maintaining your car's value.
process: Step 1: Dent evaluation under specialized lighting
Step 2: Access panel removal if needed
Step 3: Precision metal massaging
Step 4: Final polish to restore gloss

faq.question: Can you repair door edge dings?
faq.answer: Yes—we frequently fix these common Milwaukee parking mishaps. The technique differs from panel dents but often achieves near-perfect results.

faq.question: Does PDR work on aluminum panels?
faq.answer: With extra care, yes. Modern F-150s and luxury vehicles require different tools than steel, which our team is certified to handle.

services.name: Rust Repair
services.shortDescription: Stop Wisconsin's salt corrosion before it spreads.
services.description:
Milwaukee's heavy winter salting causes rust that starts small but can total a vehicle if untreated. We specialize in cutting out corrosion at its source—not just covering it up—with proper metal replacement and sealing.

Common problem areas include wheel arches on Wauwatosa's older sedans, truck beds in Brookfield, and undercarriages across the metro area. Our process involves cutting back to clean metal, welding in new sections with corrosion-resistant steel, and applying multiple protective coatings.

For classic cars stored in Bay View garages or daily drivers facing Wisconsin winters, proper rust repair adds years to your vehicle's life. We'll show you exactly where damage exists and what we can realistically preserve.
process: Step 1: Full rust inspection with borescope
Step 2: Cutting out affected metal
Step 3: Welding in replacement panels
Step 4: Epoxy primer and cavity wax application

faq.question: How much rust is too much to repair?
faq.answer: When structural components like frame rails are compromised, repairs may not be safe. We'll give you an honest assessment—sometimes prevention is better than restoration.

faq.question: Can you prevent future rust?
faq.answer: Yes—we apply oil-based cavity wax inside panels and recommend annual undercarriage washes at local Bay View shops to remove salt.

services.name: Auto Painting
services.shortDescription: Factory-quality paint jobs that withstand Wisconsin seasons.
services.description:
Milwaukee's extreme temperature swings—from humid summers to subzero winters—demand paintwork that flexes without cracking. Our climate-controlled spray booth and Wisconsin-specific paint formulations prevent the peeling and fading common with cheap repairs.

We match factory colors using a spectrophotometer, crucial for maintaining resale value on Brookfield's luxury vehicles or restoring classic Third Ward cars. Every job includes proper surface prep, multiple clear coats, and a baked finish for durability.

Whether you need a bumper respray after a Bay View fender bender or a full color change for your weekend cruiser, our lifetime warranty guarantees your paint will look new for years.
process: Step 1: Color matching with OEM formulas
Step 2: Complete surface sanding and degreasing
Step 3: Primer, base coat, and clear coat application
Step 4: 48-hour bake cycle for maximum hardness
benefits: Resistant to road salt and UV

faq.question: How long does paint need to cure?
faq.answer: While dry to touch in hours, full hardening takes 30 days. We recommend avoiding automatic car washes in Wauwatosa during this period.

faq.question: Can you match metallic flake paints?
faq.answer: Yes—our Milwaukee shop has specialized mixing equipment to replicate complex factory finishes like Mazda's Soul Red Crystal.

services.name: Frame Straightening
services.shortDescription: Precision correction for your vehicle's foundation.
services.description:
After hitting one of Milwaukee's notorious potholes or winter slide-offs, your car's frame might be bent without visible damage. Our computerized measuring system detects misalignments as small as 3mm—critical for proper wheel alignment and safety system function.

We use hydraulic pulling equipment to slowly restore factory specifications, paying special attention to unibody vehicles common in Wauwatosa's commuter traffic. Post-repair, we verify all crash sensors and driver assists work correctly.

Unlike shops that just 'pull until it looks straight,' our Milwaukee-based technicians follow manufacturer repair procedures exactly. This prevents future tire wear issues on Brookfield's winding roads and ensures airbags deploy properly if needed.
process: Step 1: Laser measurement of 32 reference points
Step 2: Hydraulic pulling with constant monitoring
Step 3: Stress relief heat treatment
Step 4: Post-repair alignment check

faq.question: How do I know if my frame is bent?
faq.answer: Signs include the car pulling to one side, unusual tire wear, or doors that don't close properly—common after Milwaukee curb impacts.

faq.question: Is frame damage always repairable?
faq.answer: Severe crumple zone damage may require replacement. We'll provide a clear assessment before starting work.

reviews.name: Mitch R.
reviews.text: Legacy cut out all the rot in my '08 Silverado's wheel wells and used corrosion-resistant panels. Two winters later with regular undercarriage washes at their recommended Bay View spot, zero rust has returned. Worth every penny.

reviews.name: Janine K.
reviews.text: After a hailstorm dented my Prius' hood last spring, Legacy had it looking new in 3 hours without any paintwork. You can't even tell which panel they worked on—and I've checked under every angle of light in my Wauwatosa garage.

reviews.name: Darnell W.
reviews.text: When a plow clipped my parked Civic in Walker's Point, Legacy handled everything with my insurance. They found hidden frame damage others would've missed and gave me a loaner for the week. Car drives straighter than before the accident.

reviews.name: Sophia L.
reviews.text: My vintage Mustang's respray looks better than the factory paint. They matched the original Wimbledon White perfectly and explained how their extra clear coats would handle Milwaukee's freeze-thaw cycles. Five stars.

reviews.name: Carlos T.
reviews.text: Hit a monster pothole on Bluemound Road that knocked my alignment out. Legacy's frame measurement showed a slight bend I wouldn't have noticed until my tires wore unevenly. Their repair fixed the pull and saved me future headaches.

reviews.name: Elena M.
reviews.text: Brookfield car washes don't remove all the winter salt, and my Subaru's quarter panels were bubbling. Legacy replaced the metal properly instead of using filler like another shop suggested. Two years later, still solid.

service-areas.title: Auto Body Service Areas | Milwaukee & Suburbs

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Auto Body proudly serves the residents of Wauwatosa, WI, providing top-quality auto-body repairs and collision services tailored to the unique needs of this historic community. With its charming 1920s-40s Tudor and Colonial homes, Wauwatosa’s residents deserve reliable auto care that matches their attention to detail. Our team understands the challenges posed by the harsh cold winters and heavy lake-effect snow, ensuring your vehicle stays safe and road-ready year-round. Whether it’s minor dents or major collision repairs, we’re here to restore your car to its original condition.
serviceAreas.localContext: Wauwatosa is known for its historic architecture and vibrant community, with a mix of residential charm and urban convenience.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Auto Body is your trusted auto-body repair shop in Brookfield, WI, catering to the upscale suburban homes and discerning drivers of this thriving community. Brookfield’s 1970s-80s neighborhoods demand precision and excellence, which is exactly what we deliver with our collision repair and paint services. The harsh winters and heavy lake-effect snow can take a toll on your vehicle, but our expert technicians are equipped to handle everything from bumper damage to full-body restorations. Trust Legacy Auto Body to keep your car looking and performing its best.
serviceAreas.localContext: Brookfield is a well-established suburb with a strong emphasis on quality living, shopping, and dining.
